title: 18-year-old and 14-year-old charged in daytime armed robbery in Charlestown
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Davonte Jordan, 18, and a 14-year-old were charged with armed robbery of an elderly woman in Charlestown, Boston.
The suspects allegedly pointed a gun, stole a purse, and fled towards Monument Square, Boston police reported.
Jordan was held without bail after pleading not guilty; the 14-year-old's identity is withheld given his age.

title: Would a Massachusetts income tax cut actually help you? Here’s what to know.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts ballot measure proposes reducing state income tax rate from 5% to 4%, potentially saving taxpayers $1,250 annually.
The tax cut could decrease state revenue by about $5.1 billion yearly, affecting funding for services like education and health.
A 2025 poll indicates 67% support for the tax reduction, though critics highlight potential negative impacts on state budgeting.

title: 3 ways Cade Cunningham’s absence could impact the Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cade Cunningham will miss time for the Pistons with a collapsed lung, affecting their playoff prospects.
Cunningham's absence could bolster Jaylen Brown's MVP chances, as Brown has met critical eligibility criteria.
The Celtics are gaining momentum with key players returning, while Detroit has seen recent struggles.

title: Readers say this is Massachusetts’ most stressful commute — and it’s not I-93 North
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston.com readers rank the Southeast Expressway as Massachusetts' most stressful commute, surpassing the Zakim Bridge/I-93 North.
Nearly half of over 150 readers cited the Southeast Expressway, with I-95/Route 128 in Waltham receiving 18% of votes.
Readers describe the Southeast Expressway as outdated, congested, and continuously jammed, irrespective of the time of day.

title: A grave situation: Medway police seek return of 12-foot-tall skeleton’s head
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Medway Police are investigating the theft of a 12-foot plastic skeleton's head, missing from Medway Recreation Center.
The skeleton, named Kevin Skellington, has been a fixture at Choate Park, decorated for holidays since September 2023.
Authorities are reviewing security footage and seek public help; contact Medway Police with information on the missing head.

title: Dropkick Murphys to play at ‘No Kings’ rally on Boston Common
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dropkick Murphys will perform at the 'No Kings' rally on Boston Common on March 28 from 2 to 4 p.m.
The rally, organized by ACLU of Massachusetts, begins at 1 p.m. and features coordinated actions nationwide.
The event marks part of the broader 'No Kings Day' movement against perceived abuses under the Trump administration.

title: Sold out show? Not if you book at this Boston hotel.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Omni Boston Hotel at the Seaport offers an All Access Pass with VIP tickets to Boston events, even if sold out.
Packages include accommodations, guaranteed event tickets, venue transportation, and optional extras like artist meet-and-greets.
Winter rates start at $300; the All Access Pass package ranges from $1,000 to $1,300 based on guest budget.

title: Six from N.H. charged in ‘brazen’ Downtown Crossing assault and robbery
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Six New Hampshire residents charged in assault and robbery at Downtown Crossing MBTA station.
Victim, 19, was attacked and robbed by group; suspects tracked via phone features and surveillance.
Suspects arraigned with bail set at $500 each, scheduled for court on May 19, authorities report.

title: Review: ‘Project Hail Mary’ borders on miraculous
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Ryan Gosling stars in 'Project Hail Mary,' a sci-fi adventure comedy directed by Phil Lord and Christopher Miller.
'Project Hail Mary' follows astronaut Ryland Grace and an alien companion as they try to save their respective planets.
Critics praise the film's humor but note its weakness in maintaining momentum toward the end of its 156-minute runtime.

title: City Council calls for more transparency from Wu over transportation projects
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council urges increased transparency from Mayor Wu regarding delayed transportation projects requiring her approval.
Council President Liz Breadon warns of potential jeopardy for $200 million in transport investments if delays persist.
Councilor Sharon Durkan seeks updates on Boston's transportation goals, last revised in the 2017 'Go Boston 2030' plan.
